candidates.why: Represents the company’s total operating revenue; clearly the top-line sales figure and preferred candidate.
This is a cost line, not revenue; should not be used for revenue despite being in the candidate list.
Finance income is non-operating and not total sales; not appropriate for REVT.
Interest income is financial income, not operating revenue; not correct for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income before Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income is a bottom-line measure, not revenue; not suitable for REVT.

variables.notes: Revenue is clearly given as 'Total operating revenue', which is the appropriate top-line figure. Other candidates are non-operating or not revenue.

candidates.why: Standard label for cost of goods sold / cost of sales and matches the COGS concept directly.
Also explicitly denotes cost of revenues/COGS and numerically matches the first 'Cost of goods sold' row; acceptable alternative but one COGS line is sufficient.
Likely contains personnel costs that may include both production and overhead; too broad to use as pure COGS when a direct COGS line exists.
Overhead/operating expenses, not direct production costs; should be treated as SG&A, not COGS.
Specific operating/rental cost; not the primary COGS measure when explicit COGS rows exist.
SG&A, not COGS.
Specific lease operating expenses; not the main COGS line given explicit COGS rows.
Appears to be a total operating expense subtotal, not just COGS.
Non-operating expense, not COGS.
Non-recurring item; not a recurring COGS line.
Balance sheet asset, not an income statement cost flow; not appropriate for COGS mapping.
Current asset; unrelated to COGS expense recognition directly.

variables.notes: There are two COGS-like rows, but one clear generic COGS label is sufficient. The explicit 'Cost of goods sold' line is chosen as the primary COGS measure.

candidates.why: Represents operating overhead; would qualify as SG&A if no explicit SG&A line existed, but an explicit SG&A line is present and populated.
Explicit SG&A line, populated in the preview; per rules, this should be used alone for XSGA_COMPONENTS when present and populated.
A specific operating expense; could be part of SG&A/operating overhead, but explicit SG&A line takes precedence.

variables.notes: An explicit and populated SG&A line exists, so only 'Selling, General & Administrative Expenses' is used for XSGA_COMPONENTS in line with the hierarchy rules.

candidates.why: Represents capitalized R&D asset balance, not period R&D expense; not appropriate for XRD which requires expense.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Accumulated amortization/impairment of R&D assets (balance sheet), not current-period R&D expense line.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Net - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing/net asset figure, not an expense line.

variables.notes: No explicit R&D expense appears on the income statement. Available R&D-related lines are balance sheet asset balances, not expenses, so XRD is treated as missing (0).

candidates.why: Primary interest expense line on the income statement; best match for interest cost.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest portion of lease liabilities; a component of total interest expense and useful as a fallback if main interest expense line is missing in some periods.
Represents allocation of net income to non-controlling shareholders, not interest expense.
Same nature as non-controlling interest; not an interest expense item.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest expense; good tertiary fallback for XINT.
Equity attributable to minority holders, not an interest expense.
Equity line for minority interests, not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: XINT is mapped to the main 'Interest Expense' line, with lease-related interest lines provided as fallbacks for periods where the main line might be missing.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent
candidates.why: Directly represents equity attributable to the parent’s shareholders, excluding non-controlling interests, which is ideal for BE.
Includes both owners’ equity and non-controlling interests; only used if a separate owners’ equity line were unavailable.
Technical balancing item, not a primary reported equity figure; not preferred when a clear equity line exists.
Balancing item, not a substantive equity measure.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing item ensuring assets=liabilities+equity; not suitable for BE.
Represents total assets; not an equity-only measure.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent

variables.notes: BE is mapped to owners’ equity excluding non-controlling interests, as required for the PROF denominator, using 'Equity attributable to owners of the parent'.

candidates.why: Represents income attributable to non-controlling interests on the income statement, but the balance sheet line is better for MIB (equity stock).
Duplicate concept on the income statement; again, BS equity line is preferred for MIB stock measure.
Explicit description of equity attributable to non-controlling interest; appropriate for MIB and similar to 'Non-controlling interests' line.
Clear balance sheet line for minority interest equity; ideal for MIB and slightly more standard terminology.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income before Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Represents comprehensive income before allocation to minorities, not the minority interest itself; not suitable for the MIB stock value.

variables.notes: MIB is taken from the balance sheet 'Non-controlling interests' line to capture the equity attributable to minority shareholders, avoiding double counting with BE.
